summary: Ransomware groups with OT capability are actively targeting North American energy infrastructure, with CISA and FBI issuing coordinated warnings. IRA-driven wind capacity expansion is outpacing security investment, creating exploitable gaps in turbine control networks.

lead_indicators.text: US executive order expands critical infrastructure designation to offshore wind, triggering mandatory cybersecurity reporting requirements
CISA issues warning on critical infrastructure reconnaissance by foreign state-sponsored actors targeting North American energy sector
Congressional hearing identifies ransomware as primary near-term cyber threat to US energy grid operators, wind farms cited as underprotected
Ransomware group targets North American energy operator, demands $22M, threatens to release proprietary turbine efficiency data
Double-extortion ransomware encrypts and exfiltrates data from wind energy predictive maintenance platform â€” 18-day recovery, full OT rebuild at three facilities
RDP exploitation identified as dominant initial access vector in North American energy sector ransomware incidents, turbine networks reachable from corporate environments
